---
consultant: Mike Johnson
prospect: Lisa Park
company: Greenfield Corp
date: 2025-01-20
duration: 18 minutes
---

**[00:00] Mike Johnson:** Hey Lisa, thanks for hopping on. So I wanted to show you our product today. Have you heard of PitchParse before?

**[00:08] Lisa Park:** Hi Mike. Um, not really. I saw the name in the meeting invite but I don't know much about it.

**[00:15] Mike Johnson:** No worries. So basically we're an AI-powered sales call analysis platform. We use AI to score your sales calls and give coaching recommendations. Pretty cool stuff. Let me share my screen and walk you through the features.

**[00:30] Lisa Park:** Sure, go ahead.

**[00:35] Mike Johnson:** Alright so here's our dashboard. You can see all your calls listed here with scores. Each call gets a score from 0 to 100. We look at things like talk-to-listen ratio, question quality, objection handling, all that good stuff. We have 10 KPIs we evaluate.

**[01:00] Lisa Park:** Ok. So what—

**[01:02] Mike Johnson:** And over here you can see the coaching recommendations. The AI looks at each call and tells your reps exactly what they need to work on. Like here, it says this rep needs to improve their discovery questions. We also generate reports that you can share with your team.

**[01:20] Lisa Park:** That sounds interesting. Can I ask—

**[01:23] Mike Johnson:** Oh and one of my favorite features is the phase timeline. We break each call into phases and show how the rep performed in each one. So you can see if they're spending too much time on the intro and not enough on discovery. It's really powerful.

**[01:40] Lisa Park:** Ok. So how does—

**[01:42] Mike Johnson:** Let me also show you the analytics page. Here you can see trends over time. This chart shows average scores by week, and this one shows score distribution. You can filter by rep, by date range, by KPI. Very flexible.

**[02:00] Lisa Park:** Mike, can I jump in for a second?

**[02:03] Mike Johnson:** Oh sorry, yeah go ahead.

**[02:05] Lisa Park:** So what exactly does your team do? Like, we're a manufacturing company. I'm not sure how sales call analysis fits into what we do. My VP just told me to take this meeting.

**[02:18] Mike Johnson:** Oh, well, do you have a sales team?

**[02:22] Lisa Park:** We have a small inside sales team, yeah. About 8 people. But most of our business comes through channel partners and trade shows.

**[02:32] Mike Johnson:** Gotcha. Well, even 8 people can benefit from call analysis. Every call matters, right? So basically our Starter plan would be perfect for a team your size. It's $99 a month and includes 50 analyses.

**[02:48] Lisa Park:** $99 a month? I don't think I have budget for new tools right now. We just went through budget cuts.

**[02:56] Mike Johnson:** Well, it's a pretty small investment when you think about it. If it helps your team close even one more deal a month, it pays for itself. What's your average deal size?

**[03:05] Lisa Park:** I'm honestly not sure. Our sales manager would know that. I'm in operations, I just support the sales team with process stuff.

**[03:15] Mike Johnson:** Oh, I see. Well, um, do you know who handles the sales tools budget?

**[03:22] Lisa Park:** That would be Tom Nguyen, our VP of Sales. He's the one who asked me to take this call actually.

**[03:30] Mike Johnson:** Ok, so Tom's the decision maker. Is there any way to get him on a call?

**[03:35] Lisa Park:** He's pretty busy. I could pass along information to him though.

**[03:40] Mike Johnson:** Sure, that works. Let me keep going through the demo then so you can share it with him. So back to the platform — we also have a coaching chat feature where managers can have AI-assisted coaching conversations...

**[04:00] Lisa Park:** Hold on, can you slow down a bit? I'm trying to take notes.

**[04:05] Mike Johnson:** Oh sure, sorry. So the coaching chat — basically your managers can ask the AI questions about a specific call and get coaching advice. It's like having an expert coach available 24/7.

**[04:20] Lisa Park:** Ok. And does this work with any phone system? We use RingCentral.

**[04:28] Mike Johnson:** Uh, we integrate with Zoom right now. I'm not sure about RingCentral actually. Let me check on that and get back to you.

**[04:38] Lisa Park:** Ok, because if it doesn't work with RingCentral that's probably a non-starter for us.

**[04:45] Mike Johnson:** Yeah, I understand. I'll definitely check on that. But even if we don't have a direct integration, you could probably export recordings and upload them manually. It's not ideal but it works.

**[04:58] Lisa Park:** That sounds like extra work. Our team wouldn't do that consistently.

**[05:05] Mike Johnson:** Fair point. Let me move on to some other features. We also have customizable KPIs, so you can set up the scoring framework to match your specific sales process. And we have report generation — the AI creates detailed coaching reports for each call.

**[05:25] Lisa Park:** Mike, I appreciate the walkthrough, but I think I need to understand better how this would actually help us. Our main problem right now is that our reps are good on the phone but terrible at follow-up. Deals stall after the first call because nobody follows up properly.

**[05:42] Mike Johnson:** Ah, well, our tool focuses more on the call itself, not the follow-up. But better calls lead to better follow-up, right? If the rep does a good job on the call, the next steps are usually clearer.

**[05:55] Lisa Park:** Maybe. But that's not really our issue. The calls are fine — our close rate on first-call-to-demo is actually pretty good. It's the post-call execution that's broken.

**[06:08] Mike Johnson:** I see. Well, we do evaluate "Next Step Quality" as one of our KPIs. So the AI would flag if a rep isn't setting up clear next steps at the end of the call. That could help with the follow-up problem.

**[06:22] Lisa Park:** That's one small piece of it. But anyway, I'll mention it to Tom. What's your pricing again?

**[06:30] Mike Johnson:** So we have three tiers. Free is $0 with 5 analyses, Starter is $99 per month with 50 analyses, and Team is $249 per month with unlimited analyses. For your team of 8, Starter would probably work. Although if Tom wants the custom KPI config, that's only on the Team plan.

**[06:52] Lisa Park:** Ok. And is there a trial?

**[06:55] Mike Johnson:** Yeah, we have a 14-day free trial on the paid plans. I can set that up for you.

**[07:00] Lisa Park:** Set it up for Tom, not me. I don't have login access to our sales tools.

**[07:06] Mike Johnson:** Right, ok. Can you send me Tom's email and I'll reach out directly?

**[07:12] Lisa Park:** I'd rather loop him in myself. Can you send me a summary email with the pricing and features, and I'll forward it to him?

**[07:20] Mike Johnson:** Sure, I can do that. What's the best email to send it to?

**[07:25] Lisa Park:** lisa.park@greenfield-corp.com. And include the thing about RingCentral integration — that'll be one of his first questions.

**[07:35] Mike Johnson:** Got it. I'll send that over today. Is there anything else you'd like me to include?

**[07:42] Lisa Park:** Not that I can think of. Just keep it concise — Tom doesn't read long emails.

**[07:48] Mike Johnson:** Understood. So, um, should I follow up next week to see if Tom's interested?

**[07:55] Lisa Park:** Give it two weeks. He's traveling next week.

**[08:00] Mike Johnson:** Ok, two weeks. I'll put that in my calendar. Thanks for your time, Lisa. Hopefully we can help the team out.

**[08:08] Lisa Park:** Sure, thanks Mike. Bye.
